Factory.title: Factory
Factory.description: Represents a manufacturing plant or supplier whose products and prices are managed in the system. Each tab in the uploaded XLSX corresponds to a factory.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Factory entity.

name.description: The name of the factory, typically derived from an XLSX sheet tab.

description.description: An optional descriptive text about the factory.

ProductGroup.title: ProductGroup
ProductGroup.description: A categorization of products used for reporting or specific PDF generation requirements, as requested for grouping 'códigos'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductGroup entity.

name.description: The name of the product group (e.g., 'Eletrodomésticos', 'Material de Escritório').

description.description: A descriptive text explaining the purpose or criteria of the product group.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a specific product with its pricing details, associated with a factory and a product group.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the product, extracted from Column A of the XLSX sheet.

unit.description: The unit of measure for the product (e.g., 'KG', 'UN', 'CAIXA'), extracted from Column B of the XLSX sheet.

factoryId.description: Reference to the Factory this product belongs to. (Relationship: Factory 1:N Product)

closedLoadPrice.description: The price of the product when purchased in a closed load quantity, extracted from Column C of the XLSX sheet.

fractionalLoadPrice.description: The price of the product when purchased in a fractional load quantity, extracted from Column D of the XLSX sheet.

productGroupId.description: Reference to the ProductGroup this product belongs to, used for PDF generation. (Relationship: ProductGroup 1:N Product)

Discount.title: Discount
Discount.description: Represents a potential discount that can be applied to products, specific to a factory. Discounts are extracted from the second 'Produto' section of the XLSX sheet.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Discount entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the discount, extracted from Column A in the discount section of the XLSX sheet.

value.description: The value of the discount, extracted from Column C in the discount section. Assumed to be a percentage (e.g., 0.10 for 10%) based on the user request 'valor do desconto possivel'.

factoryId.description: Reference to the Factory this discount is associated with. (Relationship: Factory 1:N Discount)

PriceSheetUpload.title: PriceSheetUpload
PriceSheetUpload.description: Records details of an uploaded XLSX file containing price and discount information, facilitating audit trails and reprocessing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PriceSheetUpload entity.

fileName.description: The original filename of the uploaded XLSX document.

uploadDateTime.description: The date and time when the file was uploaded.

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to the user who uploaded the file. (Relationship: User 1:N PriceSheetUpload)

status.description: The current processing status of the uploaded file (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Completed', 'Failed').

processingReport.description: A detailed report or summary of the file processing results, including any errors or warnings.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer order containing one or more products, reflecting dynamically calculated prices and discounts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

orderNumber.description: A human-readable, unique identifier for the order.

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was created.

customerName.description: The name of the customer who placed the order.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order after all calculations and discounts.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Draft', 'Submitted', 'Processing', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

notes.description: Any additional notes or comments related to the order.

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the user who created the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single product line item within an order, capturing its state (price, quantity, discounts) at the time of order creation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product being ordered. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product ordered.

loadType.description: Indicates whether the original price was based on 'closedLoad' or 'fractionalLoad' for this specific order item.

originalUnitPrice.description: The base unit price (either closedLoadPrice or fractionalLoadPrice) of the product at the time of order, *before* any order-specific discounts.

discountAmountApplied.description: The monetary amount of discount applied per unit for this specific order item, calculated at the time of order creation.

finalUnitPrice.description: The unit price of the product after applying all relevant discounts, at the time the order item was added or updated.

totalPrice.description: The total price for this order item (quantity * finalUnitPrice).

definition.description: Collection storing documents that signify a user has administrative privileges. Used for Database-Backed Access Control (DBAC). A document's ID corresponds to the user's UID. The schema for 'AdminRole' is conceptual, typically just marking existence.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier (UID) of the user who has administrative privileges.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all manufacturing plants or suppliers. These documents are read-accessible by all authenticated users and writable only by administrators.

params.name: factoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific factory.

definition.description: Top-level collection for categorizing products, primarily used for grouping 'códigos' during PDF generation. These documents are read-accessible by all authenticated users and writable only by administrators.

params.name: productGroupId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific product group.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all products. Includes 'factoryId' and 'productGroupId' for linking and querying purposes. These documents are read-accessible by all authenticated users and writable only by administrators.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific product.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all discounts. Includes 'factoryId' for linking and querying purposes. These documents are read-accessible by all authenticated users and writable only by administrators.

params.name: discountId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific discount.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific user's path, storing records of price sheet uploads. Only the 'userId' in the path (matching 'request.auth.uid') can read/write their own uploads. Includes denormalized 'uploadedByUserId' for explicit ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns these upload records.

params.name: uploadId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific price sheet upload record.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific user's path, storing orders created by that user. Only the 'userId' in the path (matching 'request.auth.uid') can read/write their own orders. Includes denormalized 'createdByUserId' for explicit ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who created these orders.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific order.

definition.description: Subcollection under a specific order, storing individual line items for that order. Access is inherited from the parent order, ensuring only the order's creator can read/write their order items.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific item within an order.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, QAPs, and Invariants. It prioritizes a secure, scalable, and easily debuggable authorization model.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **Global Data (Factories, ProductGroups, Products, Discounts):** These collections (`/factories`, `/productGroups`, `/products`, `/discounts`) are designed for organizational-level management. They are intended to be readable by all authenticated users and writable only by administrators. Crucially, there are no hierarchical authorization dependencies between these collections. Access to a `Product` document, for instance, does not depend on fetching and evaluating rules on its parent `Factory` document. The `factoryId` and `productGroupId` fields within `Product` and `Discount` documents serve purely as data relationships for querying and filtering, not for runtime authorization checks, thus eliminating the need for `get()` operations in security rules to determine access.
2.  **User-Owned Data (PriceSheetUploads, Orders, OrderItems):** For data entities owned by individual users, we employ a path-based ownership model. Collections like `/users/{userId}/priceSheetUploads` and `/users/{userId}/orders` ensure that the `userId` segment in the path directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. This design makes authorization inherently independent, as access to documents within these subcollections is determined solely by matching the authenticated user's UID with the path parameter, without requiring `get()` calls to parent documents or other collections. The `uploadedByUserId` and `createdByUserId` fields within the respective documents serve as an explicit record of ownership, reinforcing path-based authorization.

**QAPs (Query as Authorization Predicate):**
1.  **Global Data:** Collections like `/factories`, `/productGroups`, `/products`, and `/discounts` have a homogeneous security posture: all authenticated users can read them. This means any `list` operation on these collections is inherently secure, as all documents returned are authorized for the querying user. Security rules will simply allow reads if `request.auth.uid` is not null.
2.  **User-Owned Data:** The path-based structure (`/users/{userId}/...`) naturally enforces QAPs. A user can only execute `list` queries within their own `/users/{request.auth.uid}/...` path segment. Firestore security rules will enforce `request.auth.uid == userId` for these paths, ensuring that `list` operations automatically filter to only return documents the user is authorized to see.

**DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control):**
Global roles, specifically for administrators, are managed via a dedicated top-level collection: `/roles_admin/{adminId}`. The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)` grants administrative privileges. This approach allows security rules to check a user's role efficiently through a simple `exists()` check, rather than relying on custom claims or complex data structures.

**Structural Segregation:**
Data is segregated based on its security posture. Organizational-wide, read-accessible (by authenticated users) data like `Factories` and `Products` reside in top-level collections. Private, user-specific data like `PriceSheetUploads` and `Orders` are nested under `/users/{userId}/` paths, ensuring clear separation of access requirements.

**User Request Fulfillment:**
The user's request to "configurar quais codigos pertencem a qual grupo na hora de gerar o pdf" (configure which codes belong to which group when generating the PDF) is addressed by the introduction of the `ProductGroup` entity. The `/productGroups/{productGroupId}` collection allows administrators to define these groups. Each `Product` document includes a `productGroupId` field, linking products to their respective groups. This allows for flexible grouping and filtering of products for PDF generation without impacting the core security model.

Overall, this design provides a robust, clear, and scalable Firestore structure that is easy to secure and debug, adhering strictly to the mandated design principles.
